display_name: Candie Carawan and Guy Hughes Carawan (joint interview)
role_summary: Joint oral history of Guy Carawan (1927-2015) and Candie Anderson Carawan, the Highlander Folk School music teachers who taught "We Shall Overcome" to SNCC and SCLC organizers in 1960 and shaped the corpus of movement freedom songs.
ai_reading: The Carawans' top semantic neighbor is Scott Bates (entry 116, count 118), the SCLC Educational Director cohort member; second is Jamila Jones (entry 58, count 81), one of the singers who learned the freedom-song corpus at Highlander; third is Dorothy Foreman Cotton (entry 29, SCLC Educational Director); fourth is Betty Garman Robinson (entry 7, SNCC Northern support); fifth is Pete Seeger (entry 97, count 42). The Highlander music-tradition cluster the catalog has now documented across multiple entries (Pete Seeger, Jamila Jones, Scott Bates, and the Carawans themselves) is at its tightest here, the Carawans as the central node of the triadic-plus-Bates-plus-Seeger freedom-song-pedagogy signature. Their axis positions are all moderate (range -0.18 to +0.41), placing them in the catalog's centrist-voice band.
biographical_paragraph: This is a joint oral history at the Library of Congress as entry 11 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Joint interviews in the corpus combine the testimony of two or more interviewees in a single recording; the catalog page treats the embedding signature of the joint transcript as one unit (see the AI's reading above). Independent biographical sourcing for each individual interviewee is pending; future enrichment may split this entry into separate per-person pages if individual biographical material becomes accessible.

sources.title: Candie Carawan and Guy Hughes Carawan (joint interview) oral history
